metadata.title: Build a Fourier Bandwidth-Duration Uncertainty Demo with Claude Code + Manim
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = time-domain Gaussian; CRIMSON = frequency-domain FT. INK for all text.
metadata.note: Medhavy register. B00=brand intro; B01-B04=Fourier uncertainty; B05=brand outro.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) вҖ” voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' вҖ” COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B05: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' вҖ” OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Nik Bear Brown вҖ” Claude Code, Manim, and the physics, taken apart.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude вҖ” write a Manim scene, Fourier uncertainty. Gaussian pulse in time with sigma-t equals one picosecond. Show the pulse and its Fourier transform side by side. Label sigma-omega equals one over two-sigma-t equals five times ten-to-the-eleven radians per second. Three things: the rule, the numbers, what to render.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene:
  Gaussian pulse: f(t)=e^(вҲ’tВІ/2ПғВІ), Пғ_t=1ps
  show FT: F(Пү)вҲқe^(вҲ’ПүВІПғ_tВІ/2)
  label Пғ_Пү=5Г—10В№В№ rad/s
  two panels: time domain / frequency domain"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes the file. Before running вҖ” read it. Find this line: sigma-omega equals one over two times sigma-t. That is the entire time-frequency uncertainty. Check: sigma-t equals one picosecond gives sigma-omega equals five times ten-to-the-eleven radians per second. That is about eighty gigahertz of bandwidth. The product sigma-t times sigma-omega equals exactly zero-point-five вҖ” the minimum. Thirty seconds.

props.code:
# fourier_unc.py  вҖ”  Claude Code output
import numpy as np

sigma_t = 1e-12   # s  (1 picosecond)

# вҶҗ the physics: time-frequency uncertainty
sigma_w = 1 / (2 * sigma_t)  # 5e11 rad/s

t = np.linspace(-5*sigma_t, 5*sigma_t, 300)
f_t = np.exp(-t**2 / (2*sigma_t**2))

omega = np.linspace(-3*sigma_w, 3*sigma_w, 300)
F_w = np.exp(-omega**2 * sigma_t**2 / 2)

# product: sigma_t * sigma_w = 0.500 (minimum)

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two things to verify. First: the product. Sigma-t times sigma-omega equals zero-point-five вҖ” exactly the minimum uncertainty. The Gaussian is the unique pulse that achieves the lower bound. Second: the shapes. Both pulse and its transform are Gaussian вҖ” a Gaussian in time gives a Gaussian in frequency. No other shape has a smaller product. Both checks pass.

build.needs: PIPELINE вҶ’ render animated_graphics.py scene B03_*

beats.narration_text: Iterate: claude вҖ” update fourier-unc dot py вҖ” halve sigma-t to zero-point-five picoseconds. Watch sigma-omega double to ten-to-the-twelve. The time panel narrows; the frequency panel widens. One parameter. The product stays at zero-point-five. The loop closes.

props.command:
claude "update fourier_unc.py:
  вҶ’ halve Пғ_t: 1 ps вҶ’ 0.5 ps
  вҶ’ show Пғ_Пү doubles to 1e12 rad/s
  вҶ’ product Пғ_tВ·Пғ_Пү stays at 0.5"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
